prompt:
Passage 1
A regional equipment service company recently updated how it tracks parts placed on backorder. Previously, technicians wrote backordered parts on individual job sheets. This made it difficult for office staff to see all delayed parts in one place or to notify customers when expected repair dates changed.
The company now uses a shared backorder list. Each entry includes the customer name, job number, part needed, supplier, expected arrival date, and employee responsible for follow-up. Office staff review the list each morning and contact customers when a delay affects the repair schedule.
Managers report that the new process has improved communication between technicians, office staff, and customers. It also reduces the chance that a delayed part will be forgotten after the original service visit.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: A shared backorder list improved communication about delayed repair parts.
choices.B: The company stopped ordering replacement parts.
choices.C: Technicians now contact suppliers only once a year.
choices.D: Customers no longer receive repair updates.

explanation: The passage focuses on how a shared backorder list improved communication about delayed repair parts among technicians, office staff, and customers.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning April 22, employees who use the shop air hose reels must return hoses fully to the reel after use. Hoses should not be left stretched across walkways, doorways, or work areas.
The updated procedure was created after several near misses were reported involving employees stepping over or around loose hoses. The goal is to reduce tripping hazards and keep work areas clear for people carrying tools or materials.
Supervisors will review the procedure during shift meetings this week.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce a new procedure for storing air hoses after use
choices.B: To cancel all use of air hose reels
choices.C: To explain how to purchase new tools
choices.D: To assign employees to new departments

explanation: The notice announces the procedure requiring employees to return air hoses fully to the reel after use.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many organizations use equipment sign-out sheets to track who is using shared tools or devices. A sign-out sheet may include the employee’s name, equipment number, date, time borrowed, and expected return time.
This type of tracking helps prevent confusion when several employees need the same item. If a tool is missing, supervisors can check who last signed it out. If equipment is needed for a scheduled task, employees can also see whether it is already in use.
However, sign-out systems work only when employees use them consistently. If workers borrow equipment without signing it out or forget to record returns, the sheet may provide inaccurate information. Clear expectations and occasional checks help keep the system useful.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Shared equipment should never be tracked.
choices.B: Equipment sign-out sheets can reduce confusion when used consistently.
choices.C: Employees should borrow tools without recording them.
choices.D: Supervisors should avoid checking missing tools.

explanation: The passage explains that sign-out sheets help track shared equipment, but only when employees use them consistently.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before automatic doors became common in many public buildings, people usually had to pull or push heavy doors to enter. This could be inconvenient for visitors carrying packages, pushing carts, using wheelchairs, or entering with small children.
Automatic doors improved access by opening when sensors detected movement or presence near the entrance. This allowed people to enter more easily, especially when their hands were full or when physical effort was difficult. Automatic doors also helped reduce crowding at busy entrances.
Even with these advantages, automatic doors require maintenance. Sensors, tracks, motors, and safety features must be inspected to make sure the doors open and close properly.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Automatic doors improved access but still require maintenance.
choices.B: Public buildings should avoid entrances.
choices.C: Heavy doors are always easier to use.
choices.D: Sensors are unrelated to automatic doors.

explanation: The passage explains that automatic doors made entry easier but still require maintenance.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional distribution center recently added a staging area for outgoing shipments. Before the change, completed orders were placed wherever open space was available near the loading dock. During busy shifts, this sometimes made it difficult to know which orders were ready, which were waiting for paperwork, and which had already been loaded.
The new staging area is divided into marked sections. Each section is labeled by delivery route, and orders are moved there only after they pass a final check. Shipping employees scan each order when it enters the staging area and again when it is loaded onto a truck.
Supervisors report that the new layout has reduced confusion near the dock. Drivers also say it is easier to confirm that the correct orders are being loaded.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The distribution center stopped using delivery routes.
choices.B: A marked staging area improved organization for outgoing shipments.
choices.C: Drivers now decide which orders pass final checks.
choices.D: Completed orders are no longer loaded onto trucks.

explanation: The passage explains that the marked staging area improved organization and reduced confusion near the loading dock.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many companies use written checklists when shutting down equipment at the end of a workday. A shutdown checklist may include turning off power, cleaning work surfaces, recording production totals, checking guards, and reporting unusual noises or problems.
Checklists help employees avoid missing important steps, especially when they are tired or when several tasks must be completed quickly. They also create a shared expectation for what “shutdown complete” means.
A checklist should be reviewed when equipment or procedures change. If new steps are added to the work process but the checklist is not updated, employees may follow old instructions that no longer match the job.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Shutdown checklists help employees complete end-of-day tasks and must stay updated.
choices.B: Employees should shut down equipment without instructions.
choices.C: Checklists are needed only when no equipment is used.
choices.D: Old instructions always match new work processes.

explanation: The passage explains that shutdown checklists help employees complete important steps and should be updated when work changes.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities operate public boat ramps near lakes and rivers. These ramps allow residents and visitors to launch small boats, fishing boats, kayaks, or emergency response vessels.
Clear rules help keep boat ramps safe and efficient. Signs may explain parking areas, launch lanes, speed limits, life jacket reminders, and hours of use. Without clear instructions, vehicles and trailers may block access, and users may spend extra time trying to understand where to wait or unload.
Public works crews often inspect ramps for damaged pavement, slippery surfaces, debris, or low-water conditions. Regular maintenance helps keep ramps usable during busy seasons.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Public boat ramps require clear rules and maintenance to remain safe and usable.
choices.B: Boat ramps are used only by emergency crews.
choices.C: Communities should remove all signs near water.
choices.D: Kayaks cannot be launched from public ramps.

explanation: The passage explains that boat ramps need clear rules and regular maintenance to remain safe and usable.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation program recently introduced weekly “question strategy” lessons. Instructors noticed that some students knew the material but missed questions because they answered too quickly or did not compare all answer choices.
During the lessons, students practice reading the question first, locating evidence in the passage, eliminating clearly wrong choices, and then choosing the best answer. Instructors explain that the correct answer should be supported by the text, not just sound reasonable.
The lessons are short, but instructors repeat the same process each week. The goal is to help students build a habit they can use during the timed entrance exam.

Why did the program introduce question strategy lessons?

choices.A: Some students missed questions because they answered too quickly or failed to compare choices.
choices.B: Students no longer needed reading passages.
choices.C: Instructors wanted to remove answer choices.
choices.D: The entrance exam no longer included timing.

explanation: The lessons were added because some students missed questions by answering too quickly or not comparing all answer choices.

Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation program recently introduced weekly “question strategy” lessons. Instructors noticed that some students knew the material but missed questions because they answered too quickly or did not compare all answer choices.
During the lessons, students practice reading the question first, locating evidence in the passage, eliminating clearly wrong choices, and then choosing the best answer. Instructors explain that the correct answer should be supported by the text, not just sound reasonable.
The lessons are short, but instructors repeat the same process each week. The goal is to help students build a habit they can use during the timed entrance exam.

According to the passage, the correct answer should be:

choices.A: The longest answer choice
choices.B: The answer that sounds most familiar
choices.C: Supported by the text
choices.D: Chosen without comparison

explanation: The passage states that the correct answer should be supported by the text.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation program recently introduced weekly “question strategy” lessons. Instructors noticed that some students knew the material but missed questions because they answered too quickly or did not compare all answer choices.
During the lessons, students practice reading the question first, locating evidence in the passage, eliminating clearly wrong choices, and then choosing the best answer. Instructors explain that the correct answer should be supported by the text, not just sound reasonable.
The lessons are short, but instructors repeat the same process each week. The goal is to help students build a habit they can use during the timed entrance exam.

Which statement is best supported by the passage?

choices.A: Repeated strategy practice can help students build useful test-taking habits.
choices.B: Students should avoid looking for evidence.
choices.C: Comparing answer choices usually makes answers less accurate.
choices.D: Timed exams do not require decision-making strategies.

explanation: The repeated weekly process is intended to help students build a useful habit for the timed exam.
